folio.label: 150v
folio.l: ¢ Here bigynnetú the Shipmans tale , next folwyngö the Pardoner

note.notetxt: IRthe rubric is written in the header, and it seems to be the same ink of the text. Double chcek this comment.Est/ODR

folio.l: A Marchaunt whilom dwelled , at Seynt Denys
That riche was , for whicú men helde hi~ wys
A wyf he hadde , of excellent beaute
And compaignable , and reuelous was she
Whicú is a thyngö , that causetú more dispence
Than wortú is , al the chere and reuäence
That men hem doon , at festes and at daunces
Swicú salutacions , and countenaunces
Passen , as dotú a shadewe vp on the wal
But woo is hi~ , that paien moot for al
The sely housbond , algate he mot paie
He moot vs clothe , and he moot vs arraie
Al for his owene worshipe , richely
In whicú array , we dauncen iolily
And if that he nougút may , ¹auenture
Or elles lust nat , swicú dispence endure
But thynketú , it is wasted and I lost
Thanne moot a nother , paien for oure cost
Or lene vs gold , and that is ¹ilous
This noble Marchau~t , held a worthy hous
For whicú he hadde alday , so gret repair
For his largesse , and for his wyf was fair
That wonder is , but herkenetú to my tale
Amonges alle theise gestes , grete and smale
There was a Monk , a fair man and a bold
I trowe a thretty wynter , he was old
That euäe in oon , was drawyngö to that place
This ©onge Monk , that was so fair of face
A queynted was so , witú the goode man
Sithe that here first , knewelicú , bygan
That in his hous , as familier was he
As it is possible , any frend to be
And for as mechel , as this goode man
And eke this Monk , of whicú that I bigan
Were bothe two I born~ , in oo village
The Monk hi~ cleymetú , as for cosynage
And he a geyn , he seitú nat oones nay
But was as glad ther of , as foule of day
For to his herte , it was a gret plesaunce
Thus ben they knyt , witú eterne alliaunce
And eche of hem , gan other for to assure
Of bretherhede , while that here lif may dure
¢ Free was Daun Ioún , and namely of dispence
